items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, youth empowerment programs, and library facilities improvements. These updates signal the city's direction on investing in youth services and public library infrastructure for residents.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing for 333 Franklin Street postponed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22, 2026. The project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed when a new hearing date is confirmed.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully outlined in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what this construction project entails and where it will be located in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project advances with consultant extension
items.summary: The city extended its consultant agreement with DKS Associates to continue work on the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project addresses safety at the rail crossing on a major commuter corridor.

items.headline: Street pavement replacement project contract awarded for 2026
items.summary: The city council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will resurface deteriorating streets across San José.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including edits to how projects will be evaluated and scored. This plan affects how the city prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements in future development.

items.headline: Council Vacancy Rules Changed to Reduce Council Size
items.summary: Milpitas City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled, with a change that would reduce the overall size of the council. This affects how future vacancies will be filled and the structure of city government.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9 million senior housing development with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's growing need for affordable senior living. This represents significant new housing supply for an underserved demographic in the tight local market.
